zh.lifeSummary: 當Richard de la Haye於1123年出生在英國林可爾史爾布拉特萊比時，他的父親Robert de la Haye0歲，母親Muriel Lincoln39歲。他於1146年與Matilda de Vernon Lady of Varenguebec在英國英格蘭林可爾史爾結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和4個女兒的父母。他去世於1169年5月7日在他的家鄉享年46歲，埋葬在英國林可爾史爾斯瓦頓。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Richard de la Haye于1123年出生在英国林可爾史爾布拉特萊比时，他的父亲Robert de la Haye0岁，母亲Muriel Lincoln39岁。他于1146年与Matilda de Vernon Lady of Varenguebec在英国英格兰林可爾史爾结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和4个女儿的父母。他去世于1169年5月7日在他的家乡享年46岁，埋葬在英国林可爾史爾斯瓦頓。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: la
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Hung, Cuong, Anh, Thanh, Ha, Hoang, Tuan, Chau, Hao, Hien, Hoa, Hue, Nam, Phong, Sinh, Tuong, Min, Tam, Tanh, Thai, Tham, Tinh, Yeong.  Chinese Hong, Hang, Sang, Tong, Chan, Chi, Hwa Ja, Hyung, Kam, Ki Bong, Kin, Man.
Vietnamese: from the Chinese surname 羅, see   Luo   1.
Vietnamese (Lã): from the Chinese surname 呂, see Lu 6 and 3 below.

variants.name: Luo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Jian, Yi, Ping, Yong, Zhen, Feng, Hong, Ming, Jin, Bin, Chen, Hai, Lan, Min, Huan, Hung, Shen, Tian.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 羅, meaning ‘net for catching birds’ or ‘to collect’ in Chinese: (i) from Luo (羅), the name of an ancient state (located in present-day Hubei province). After it was annexed by the state of Chu during the early Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ), people from the state migrated southwards and adopted Luo (羅) as their surname. (ii) from the Po Duo Luo (破多羅) and Chi Luo (叱羅) families from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China, who changed their original surnames to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) from the personal name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅), leader of an ancient Turkic tribe who are believed to have migrated to China during the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ). His descendants inherited his name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅) as their surname and shortened it to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iv) from the name of one of the seven families from the Bandun Man, an ethnic group residing in ancient southwestern China (located mainly in present-day Sichuan province).

nameMeanings.name: Haye
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Hans, Helmut, Willi.

North German, East Frisian, and Dutch: from a personal name, Frisian Hajo, ancient Germanic Haio; or from a short form of the female name Hadewig, composed of the elements hadu ‘battle, strife’ + wīg ‘war’.
German: variant of   Hey   4, 5.

variants.name: Deshayes
meanings: French: topographic name for someone who lived by an enclosure, from the plural of Old French haye ‘hedge’ (see Haye and Hayes ), with fused preposition and definite article des ‘from the’; or a habitational name denoting someone from Les Hayes, the name of several places in various parts of France. Compare Deshaies .

variants.name: Hayenga
meanings: East Frisian: patronymic from the personal name Hajo (see Haye 1). It is formed with the suffix -enga, a variant of -inga (from the patronymic suffix -ing and genitive plural ending -a).

variants.name: Hey
meanings: English (Yorkshire and Lancashire): variant of   Hay  .
Dutch: variant, mostly Americanized (or archaic) and Flemish, of Heij, a topographic name for someone who lived on a heath, Dutch hei, heide.
German: metonymic occupational name for a grower or mower of grass, from Middle High German höu ‘grass, hay’.

variants.name: Hayen
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Alphonse.  German Heinz, Wilhelm.
Flemish, East Frisian, and North German: patronymic from   Haye   1.

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rickert
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

en.lifeSummary: When Richard de la Haye was born in 1123, in Brattleby, Lincolnshire, England, his father, Robert de la Haye, was 0 and his mother, Muriel Lincoln, was 39. He married Matilda de Vernon Lady of Varenguebec in 1146, in Lincolnshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 4 daughters. He died on 7 May 1169, in his hometown, at the age of 46, and was buried in Swaton, Lincolnshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Richard de la Haye est né en 1123, à Brattleby, Lincolnshire, Angleterre, son père, Robert de la Haye, avait 0 ans et sa mère, Muriel Lincoln, avait 39 ans. Il a épousé Matilda de Vernon Lady of Varenguebec en 1146, à Lincolnshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 4 filles. Il est décédé le 7 mai 1169, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 46 ans, et a été enterré à Swaton, Lincolnshire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Richard de la Haye 1123 in Brattleby, Lincolnshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Robert de la Haye 0 und seine Mutter Muriel Lincoln 39. Er hat 1146 in Lincolnshire, England, United Kingdom Matilda de Vernon Lady of Varenguebec geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 4 Töchter. Er starb am 7. Mai 1169 im Alter von 46 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort und wurde in Swaton, Lincolnshire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Richard de la Haye è nato il nel 1123 nel Brattleby, Lincolnshire, England, il suo padre, Robert de la Haye, aveva 0 anni e il suo madre, Muriel Lincoln, aveva 39 anni. Lui sposato Matilda de Vernon Lady of Varenguebec nel 1146, nel Lincolnshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 4 figlie. Morì il 7 maggio 1169, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 46 anni, e fu sepolto a Swaton, Lincolnshire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Richard de la Hayeが1123年にイギリスリンコルンシュアイアブラットレビーで生まれた時，父Robert de la Hayeは0歳，母Muriel Lincolnは39歳であった。1146年にイギリスイギリスリンコルンシュアイアでMatilda de Vernon Lady of Varenguebecと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。彼は1169年5月7日に故郷で46歳で死去し，イギリスリンコルンシュアイアスワトンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Richard de la Haye(이)가 1123년 영국 링코실르 브래틀레비에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Robert de la Haye(은)는 0세였고, 그의 어머니 Muriel Lincoln(은)는 39세였다. 그는 1146년 영국 잉글랜드 링코실르에서 Matilda de Vernon Lady of Varenguebec(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1169년 5월 7일 그의 고향에서 46세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 링코실르 스왓에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Richard de la Haye nasceu em 1123, em Brattleby, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Robert de la Haye, tinha 0 anos e sua mãe, Muriel Lincoln, tinha 39 anos. Ele casou-se com Matilda de Vernon Lady of Varenguebec em 1146, em Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 4 filhas. Ele faleceu em 7 de maio de 1169, em sua cidade natal, com 46 anos, e foi sepultado em Swaton, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Richard de la Haye родился в 1123 г. в местности браттлеби, линколншир, Англия, его отцу по имени Robert de la Haye было 0 лет, а матери, которую звали Muriel Lincoln, 39. Он женился на женщине по имени Matilda de Vernon Lady of Varenguebec в 1146 г. в местности линколншир, Англия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 4 дочери. Он умер 7 мая 1169 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 46 лет и был похоронен в местности сватон, линколншир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Richard de la Haye nació en 1123, en Brattleby, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Robert de la Haye, tenía 0 años y su mamá, Muriel Lincoln, tenía 39 años. Se casó con Matilda de Vernon Lady of Varenguebec en 1146, en Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 4 hijas. Murió el 7 de mayo de 1169, en su pueblo, a la edad de 46 años, y fue sepultado en Swaton, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra.
